skill.name: core-components
skill.description: Guides UI work with core components, design tokens, layout patterns, and component prop conventions.
skill.summary: Use core components and design tokens to build consistent UI screens.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick and weak crypto patterns, but review found Markdown TSX examples and dollar-prefixed design tokens, not executable Ruby or cryptographic code. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, secret handling, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from documentation-only code examples.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks occur in Markdown TSX code fences and inline design token names such as spacing and color tokens. They document UI component usage and do not execute shell commands or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown examples, not runnable skill scripts. No command execution API, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction appears in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts map to ordinary prose and component documentation, including the skill description and token guidance. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review found UI guidance and token examples at the cited lines. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Teams lose consistency when screens use raw components and hard-coded style values. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use core components, design tokens, and shared layout patterns.
actual_capabilities: Recommends core components such as Box, Text, Button, Input, Card, HStack, and VStack.
Shows token-based spacing, color, and typography patterns for TSX interfaces.
Provides layout examples for screens, forms, list items, and content cards.
Identifies anti-patterns such as hard-coded values, raw platform components, and inline styles.
Defines component prop patterns that accept token values and variants.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include runnable component source files.
It assumes the project already has matching core components and design tokens.
It does not validate generated UI against a live design system package.
It does not cover accessibility rules beyond component consistency.

use_cases.title: Standardize Feature Screens
use_cases.description: Use the skill to convert feature screens from raw layout and style values to shared components and tokens.

use_cases.title: Review UI Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Use the guidance to find hard-coded spacing, raw platform components, and inline styles before merge.

use_cases.title: Document Component Usage
use_cases.description: Use the examples to explain approved component patterns for forms, lists, cards, and screen layouts.

prompt_templates.title: Use Core Components
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Build this screen using the core component patterns. Use Box, Text, Button, Input, HStack, and VStack where appropriate. Use design tokens for spacing, colors, and typography.

prompt_templates.title: Replace Hard-Coded Styles
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this UI file and replace hard-coded spacing, color, and typography values with approved design tokens from the core components guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Token-Based Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable component that follows the component props pattern. Accept token-based padding, variant options, and children. Use core components internally.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a UI Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this UI flow for design system compliance. Identify raw platform components, inline styles, hard-coded values, and missing core component patterns. Recommend precise fixes.

output_examples.input: Create a profile summary card with a title, subtitle, avatar, and action button.
output_examples.output: A component plan that uses Card, CardHeader, CardBody, HStack, VStack, Avatar, Text, and Button with token-based spacing and semantic colors.

output_examples.input: Review this form layout for design system compliance.
output: Replace raw text and view elements with core Text, Box, and VStack components.
Use spacing tokens for gaps and padding instead of numeric values.
Use Input error props for validation feedback instead of custom inline styles.

output_examples.input: Design a list item pattern for a settings screen.
output_examples.output: A layout recommendation using HStack for horizontal structure, VStack for text grouping, semantic text tokens, and a trailing icon for navigation.

best_practices: Prefer core components over raw platform components for layout, text, buttons, inputs, and cards.
Use semantic design tokens for spacing, color, typography, borders, and component variants.
Keep reusable component props constrained to approved token and variant values.
anti_patterns: Do not hard-code spacing, colors, typography sizes, or font weights in UI components.
Do not mix raw platform components with core components when a core equivalent exists.
Do not add inline styles for common layout and visual states already covered by tokens.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build UI with a shared core component library, design tokens, and consistent TSX layout patterns.

faq.question: Does the skill include component source code?
faq.answer: No. It provides usage guidance and examples for projects that already have matching components.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill for refactoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide refactors from raw components and hard-coded styles to token-based core components.

faq.question: Which UI areas does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers spacing, color, typography, layout stacks, text, buttons, inputs, cards, screen layouts, forms, and list items.

faq.question: Does it define exact token values?
faq.answer: It lists common spacing and typography token examples, plus semantic color token names and their intended uses.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found documentation-only examples and no executable commands, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection attempts.
